prompts_findings.prompt_lead: Draw the agent loop as an ASCII diagram. Name the harness, the LLM, the tool-call s

risks_fired.evidence: Four named sub-elements may prompt a brief 'I'll diagram these four parts' opening, but single-output request, no planning required — preamble is minimal.
risks_fired.fix_hint: Optional body primer ('Claude may open with a framing sentence — skip to the diagram') if delivery experience shows disruption.

prompts_findings.prompt_lead: List the tools you have available in this session. One line each.
Explain how you know what tools you can call in this session. Then explain how MCP-con

risks_fired.evidence: 'Then explain...' two-part structure invites brief framing before diving in; below the 4+-sub-step structural trigger threshold.
risks_fired.fix_hint: One-line body primer after the fence if preamble disrupts flow in delivery; not blocking.

risks_fired.evidence: Claude explains its own tool-injection mechanism; minor over-confidence risk on introspective account. Student's Prompt 2 output provides immediate ground truth.
risks_fired.fix_hint: No fix needed; Prompt 2 list serves as natural verification anchor.
